Perk Text: The character has an inherent sense of style, whether it is in their gear or their own skill or even in a way they use their power. They could inherently make better-looking stuff, be able to tailor their attitude and actions to be more charming, or something else as befits the character.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Love, compassion, creativity, overwhelming emotion

Reversed: Blocked or repressed emotions

Perk Text: Maintains close romantic or familial ties to unpowered individual; individual supports one's strengths from the background, whether by offering skills or keeping the rest of the character's life running smoothly.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Unified love, partnership, attraction, relationships

Reversed: Break-up, imbalance in a relationship, lack of harmony

Perk Text: Belongs to a moderate-sized group that offers resources or contacts, or such a group naturally and gradually forms with character at the center. The cape can utilise their contacts to gain information about happenings within the city, acquire assets such as weapons, or otherwise.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Celebration, friendship, creativity, community

Reversed: An affair, “three’s a crowd”, stifled creativity

Perk Text: A great opportunity faces the character, and they simply need to take advantage of that. Taking advantage of this opportunity may be risky, but it comes with commensurate benefits. The nature of this opportunity is dependent on the character's history and motives.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Meditation, contemplation, apathy, re-evaluation

Reversed: Boredom, missed opportunity, being aloof

Perk Text: A mentor, patron, or someone close to the character is now gone, but they left the door open, or a resemblance to someone more powerful garnered opportunity. They could start with or gain assets, mooks, a bonus to reputation gains, or a constant stream of minor benefits.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Loss, regret, disappointment, despair, bereavement

Reversed: Moving on, acceptance, forgiveness

Perk Text: The character has had good days, whether because they were raised well or they had time after their trigger event to get their feet and meet good people. In a rough spot, they can tap into this optimism to get a small boost - a minor ally, a small windfall, something fitting that particular character.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Reunion, nostalgia, childhood memories, innocence

Reversed: Stuck in the past, naivety, unrealistic

Perk Text: The character has access to resources that allow them to pretend to be someone else effectively. This can be high-quality fake identification, a body double, a particular set of skills, et cetera. Using this resource can cost the character, or it may fall apart if used too often.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Fantasy, illusion, wishful thinking, choices, imagination

Reversed: Temptation, illusion, diversionary tactics

Perk Text: The character is especially disciplined in managing their approach to life. This can manifest as bonuses to keeping their civilian and cape identities apart, increased ability to train their skill proficiencies, or other benefits as befits the character and the way they manage their approach to life.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Escapism, disappointment, abandonment, withdrawal

Reversed: Hopelessness, aimless drifting, walking away

Perk Text: Something holds the character back in a positive way, and woe betide whatever disrupts that. This 'touchstone' keeps the character grounded, stopping them from going overboard, making sure they remember essential parts of their humanity, et cetera. When lost, the character gains a benefit strictly to recoup or take revenge.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Wishes fulfilled, comfort, happiness.

Reversed: Greed, dissatisfaction, materialism.

Perk Text: This character stands for something - not necessarily something good or right, but something. They gain an edge when dealing with those that go against them in that department, generally a level of inhuman determination. Success has a Fisher King effect; the character is a symbol to an extent, causing change, drawing followers, et cetera. Causes include something as abstract as a tone (fear amongst villains), an ideology (religion, nihilism, socialism) or concept (war, nature).
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Harmony, marriage, happiness, alignment

Reversed: Misalignment, broken home.

Perk Text: This character has their thumb on the pulse of some pipeline of information or another.  A villain might have a family member that works for the PRT office, a hero might have a family member that works as a career henchman.  Save for the most secret information, the character knows what's up.  If they already do know most of what's going on, the source might provide details on surprise attacks, ambushes, or general activities of the other side.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: A messenger, creative beginnings.

Reversed: Emotional immaturity, creative block.

Perk Text: The character is exceedingly good-looking or very stylish, benefiting them across the board. In social situations, they fare better, being either naturally charming or having a grasp of skills that enhance their ability to leave positive impressions.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Romance, charm, knight in shining armor, imagination.

Reversed: Unrealistic, jealousy, moodiness.

Perk Text: The character, either through study or natural skill, has their finger on other's emotional pulse. They can assess others with greater skill, even learning their life flaw or flaws, gain an understanding of another's attitudes towards the other people in the area, or something else as befits the character.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Emotional security, calm, intuitive, compassionate.

Reversed: Emotional insecurity, co-dependency.

Perk Text: The character has a certain commanding presence or skill to them that lends itself well to leadership. Higher-quality underlings, increased numbers of mooks, a loyal and/or competent assistant, or otherwise as fits the character. Could alternatively increase their ability to lead a group of player characters rather than mooks.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Emotional balance and control, generosity.

Reversed: Emotional manipulation, volatility.

Perk Text: The character has access to a deep reserve of money, but it's not entirely up to them how its used. Depending on what the money is - trust fund, drug income, job bonuses - it becomes easier or harder to utilise for various purchases depending on how suitable they are for the specific type of wealth.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Manifestation, new financial opportunity

Reversed: Lost opportunity, lack of planning/foresight.

Perk Text: Whether by legitimate means or otherwise, the character has secured themselves in a high quality job. This job allows them to draw a sizable income without being stressed out, and yet pursue a cape life on the side. May include non-job situations like someone pulling in money from a lawsuit. If this does not fit, the character may be able to call in favours due to their work environment or gain resources to help in their cape life.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Balance, adaptability, time management.

Reversed: Disorganization, financial disarray.

Perk Text: The character habitually cleans their equipment, has an eye for high-quality goods, or has skill in keeping their stuff in good shape. In any case, the character's equipment is top of the line, tougher, harder to snatch and disarm. Alternatively, the character is able replace lost gear with stuff of similar quality.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Control, stability, security, possession.

Reversed: Greed, materialism, self-protection.

Perk Text: The character can do more with less. Either their gear is custom-made and they're more familiar with using it, their past gives them a measure of skill in making the most out of the resources they have, or they can operate effectively alone if necessary.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Isolation, insecurity, worry, financial loss.

Reversed: Recovery, spiritual poverty.

Perk Text: The character understands the systems and rules which their city and society run on, and can exploit that. Using this, they are, to an extent, able to sculpt their own reputation, and perhaps even others'. Shaking an association they would rather not have, shifting the blame to other possible targets, et cetera.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Generosity, charity, prosperity, sharing.

Reversed: Debt, selfishness, one-sided charity.

Perk Text: The character has positioned themselves well to reap later returns.  Favors are owed, money invested, inheritances due. They may be able to invest resources - mostly cash - and convert that to a greater amount of assets that would be useful for the cape's life. Guns, costumes, cash, other assets that can be discussed with the GM.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Vision, perseverence, profit, reward.

Reversed: Lack of vision, limited success or reward

Perk Text: The character knew an individual in the cape scene while growing up or for a major part of their life. They have superior knowledge of how to outfit their cape life, have a favour or two with a NPC cape, know a few secrets relevant to cape life, or something else.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Apprenticeship, education, quality.

Reversed: Perfectionism, lacking ambition or focus.

Perk Text: The character, by whatever means suits them, is in possession of some form of business endeavor. This can be wholly civilian, or based on the characteristics of their power. While this is a potential weakness an enemy can target, the character reaps a number of financial, material, and social benefits for upkeeping this business.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Security, power, control, discipline, abundance.

Reversed: Authoritative, controlling, domineering.

Perk Text: When the character wins, the character wins big. At the point of a major success, the character may gain an additional minor reward, depending on the nature of the success. A public victory such as taking down a major villain, for example, could result in gaining a monetary reward as donations or bounty money, while successfully creating a group could draw followers.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Raw power, victory, break-throughs, mental clarity.

Reversed: Confusion, chaos, lack of clarity.

Perk Text: The character has a complicated mentality, and this is channeled positively, so to speak. It’s harder to get a read on this character, whether with mundane skill or powers. Alternatively, the character has a presence to them that can be used to threaten and unnerve, or in the off chance they meet those with similarly twisted mindsets, inspire and lead.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Indecision, choices, truce, stalemates, blocked emotion.

Reversed: Indecision, confusion, information overload.

Perk Text: The character has a superior headquarters, bought, found, or inherited. It is outfitted with some, if not all of the following: barracks for underlings, workshops, a business front, security measures, top-notch computer hardware, et cetera. They may alternatively be on the way to secure such a headquarters of their choice.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Contemplation, recuperation, passivity, relaxation.

Reversed: Restlessness, burn-out, lack of progress.

Perk Text: The character covers ground where it counts. They may have a deeper knowledge of how to move about a city, a strong grasp of back alleys and shortcuts, training in long-distance chases, experience in getting around and above obstacles, or otherwise. Usually benefits in an extended chase, race, or other similar events.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Opinionated, hasty, action-oriented, communicative.

Reversed: Scattered thought, disregard for consequences.

Perk Text: Most people don't have the time or motivation to create, but this character is not most people. In one field, they have both talent and practice, and are thus able to create something of moderate value. Custom-fitted armour, artwork, et cetera. The specifics of what they can make depends on the character and their history.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Inspiration, power, creation

Reversed: Delays, lack of motivation, burdens.

Perk Text: This character joins the fray with pretty much all the gear someone could want, and can even supply underlings or team members. They could have a supplier of weaponry - even illegal firearms - legacy gear from a dead cape, access to some sort of warehouse of equipment, or otherwise.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Enterprise, expansion, foresight.

Reversed: Delays, obstacles to long-term plans.

Perk Text: Through reputation, luck, old contacts or natural charisma, the character has a larger-than-usual collection of fans. Generally, these fans are 'useless mooks', with the caveat that they are almost insanely loyal. They are likely to attempt to support the character where they can, be it a free car ride or stalking an enemy. Recruiting from this pool of fans is far easier than recruiting off the street.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Celebration, home, community.

Reversed: Breakdown in communication, transitions.

Perk Text: The character maintains a business relationship with another organisation, faction, or powerful individual. Through this, they may be afforded a level of renown in specific circles, be able to purchase highly specific goods, maintain some level of entrepreneur income.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Disagreement, competition, strife, tension and conflict.

Reversed: Conflict avoidance, diversity, unhappy compromise.

Perk Text: The character bounces back from their worst moments to a degree, softening the blows. Upon an utter loss, hitting their darkest hour, et cetera, the character can come across a minor bonus to build themselves back up. This benefit generally does not help revert the loss, acting as something of a consolation prize.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Public recognition, victory, self-confidence.

Reversed: Egotism, disrepute, lack of confidence, fall from grace.

Perk Text: The character has easy, if not free access to an exotic vehicle of their choice. Common choices are mil-spec trucks, helicopters, yachts, though the character's backstory must fit their vehicle of choice. Access to this vehicle may also come with the requisite skill or a mook who can drive the vehicle.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Speed, action, air travel, movement, swift change.

Reversed: Delays, frustration, procrastination.

Perk Text: Something the character was involved in has become a point of notability or notoriety. They may have defeated a notable cape early in their career, had a reputation in their career, et cetera. This grants them some reputation and cachet with low-level rabble and mooks, dealing with crowds.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Burden, responsibility, hard work and stress.

Reversed: taking on too much, avoiding responsibility.

Perk Text: The character has access to resources that enhance their learning new skills, such as tutors, a stock of books, or even just innate potential. Starting new mental tasks is easier for them, they're rewarded for knowing a variety of disparate subjects, or they can easily pick up a low-level understanding of more topics and skills.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Energy, passion, lust, action, adventure, impulsiveness.

Reversed: Haste, scattered energy, frustration

Perk Text: Most people muddle along in life, but not this character. They are a source of light and energy, the natural "heart" of a group. They may gain benefits when dealing with groups of people, be able to reconcile two conflicting opinions in a team, or more as fits the character. The loss of this character can galvanise allies against whatever took them down.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Exuberance, warmth, vibrancy, determination

Reversed: Shrinking violet, aggressive, demanding

Perk Text: The character had potential and capitalized on it. The player picks a life perk and gains it.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Innocence, spontaneity, free spirit, potential.

Reversed: Naivety, foolishness, risk-taking.

Perk Text: A singular field has captured the character's attention, and within this field their skills advance fast and far. This can reward the character for focusing their mental efforts, or grant them resources to train up specific skills in their fields, et cetera. In short, the character learns narrow but deep.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Power, skill, concentration, action

Reversed: Manipulation, poor planning, latent talents

Perk Text: The character maintains favors they can call in with low level organizations or a single favor with a high level organization. Smaller favours range from equipment, assets, limited information, minor allies, while high level favours can be anything from a secure bunker to a strike team to rare Tinkertech. Can include a way to gain more favours by working to further the organisation's goals.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Fertility, femininity, beauty, nature

Reversed: Creative block, dependence on others

Perk Text: Where others may give up or break down, this character just refuses to let bad shit stop them. At the darkest moments, they're the one who makes the inspiring speeches, gets the group back on its feet. They may cultivate a reputation for being reliable, someone who will pull through in whatever situation is thrown at them.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Strength, courage, patience, control, compassion

Reversed: Weakness, self-doubt, lack of discipline

Perk Text: Like Icarus with his wings, rising too high too fast comes at a cost. Roll again for life perk. Double down on the strength of that perk. Roll three times for a life disadvantage. GM vetoes one and player vetoes one. Take remaining pick.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Good luck, karma, life cycles, destiny, a turning point

Reversed: Bad luck, negative external forces, out of control

Perk Text: The character was once offered a different path in life, and that option still remains open. After a critical moment in their story, they may change a major facet of their identity - adopt a new cape persona and alignment, for example. This is not a perfect change, with the nature of what they were and what they now are informing any potential downsides to the new identity. An ex-villain, for example, would be more heavily restricted than a new heroic cape.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: suspension, restriction, letting go, sacrifice

Reversed: Martyrdom, indecision, delay

Perk Text: The character had, is undergoing, or is due to undergo a transition in life, leaving behind their old life to start anew. An old job, a family with assets, education, training, something was given up for their new lifestyle. Their old skills, contacts, assets, or training come into play in their new life. Some ways this manifests are income, loyal contacts or goons, unique assets, skill pips.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Endings, beginnings, change, transformation, transition

Reversed: Resistance to change, unable to move on.

Perk Text: The character has a grasp of the troubles that plague people, and something of a handle on their own issues. They can work off their own life flaws, eventually removing it. They may also be able to exacerbate or ameliorate the flaws of others. Requires GM approval.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Balance, moderation, patience, purpose

Reversed: Imbalance, excess, lack of long-term vision

Perk Text: The character has an ally, though not a strong one. This might be a sidekick or unpowered ally but will accompany the character. They may have power or life perks and/or flaws. Should the primary character perish or be taken out of combat, the player may, at GM's discretion, resume play as their 'bud'.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Bondage, addiction, sexualism, materialism

Reversed: Detachment, breaking free, power reclaimed

Perk Text: After suffering a major loss, the character bounces back hard and with a vengeance. They scrounge up assets specifically slated for a second encounter, locate allies to help with the rematch, learn of weaknesses that they could exploit, or something else. In any case, the character is tenacious and can exact vengeance easier.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Disaster, upheaval, sudden change, revelation

Reversed: Avoiding disaster, fear of change

Perk Text: The character's goals are clear, their path is lit, and their beliefs are aligned. When working towards a milestone goal, the character is more confident, convincing, driven to succeed, amongst other possible minor benefits. Alternatively, they gain more control over the rewards they gain from completing a milestone, rolling two life perks and picking one.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Hope, spirituality, renewal, serenity

Reversed: Lack of faith, despair, discouragement

Perk Text: The character understands the mind, theirs and others, and they can put that to great effect. They may be able to glean clues into the insecurities and breaking points of others, have a safety net for their own rock-bottom moments, or otherwise as fits the character.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Illusion, fear, anxiety, insecurity, subconscious

Reversed: Release of fear, unhappiness, confusion

Perk Text: The character is in their physical prime, and they use this to great effect. This allows the character to carry out physical feats as appropriate to a selected stat. Lifting massive weights, long-distance sprints, deft manuveurs, et cetera. Utilising these feats strains the character, but recovery is relatively fast.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Fun, warmth, success, positivity, vitality

Reversed: Temporary depression, lack of success

Perk Text: Death is not the end, but the start of a new story. At the character's death, the player gets a new character which gains powers and picks up the mantle of their old one. A bud, a triggered minion, a family member, significant other, et cetera. This new character inherits some benefits, such as money, some connections, but may also need to deal with the responsibilities their progenitor left behind.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Judgement, rebirth, inner calling, absolution

Reversed: Self doubt, refusal to look inward
